title: RuntimeAdapter contract + ClaudeCodeAdapter reference + conformance harness (the runtime polymorphism the pool/bridge build on)

tests.summary: 4 passed — runAdapterConformance drives a StubAdapter through spawn→awaitReady→sendTurn→awaitResponse→teardown (all ok, spawned=1/torndown=1); awaitResponse returns per-turn text (the hardest primitive); conformance fails loudly when a method is missing; the ClaudeCodeAdapter reference implements every ADAPTER_METHODS method. (adapter-contract-ok)

build.summary: unchanged — packages/claude-pty .mjs addition + test, not in the Next bundle

e2e.summary: N/A — contract + conformance harness; the committed conformance test (stub adapter, no live model) is the re-runnable gate.

notes: packages/claude-pty/src/runtime-adapter.mjs defines the runtime-agnostic RuntimeAdapter contract (spawn/awaitReady/sendTurn/awaitResponse/setModel/setEffort/resume/teardown) — sendTurn + awaitResponse are split deliberately because turn-boundary detection (knowing a TUI turn FINISHED by scraping output) is the HARDEST per-adapter primitive (the brief's flagged main risk for Codex/Gemini). ClaudeCodeAdapter is the reference (both faces): wraps OperativePtySession (claude-pty), sendTurn stores the in-flight runTurn promise, awaitResponse resolves {text, artifacts}, setModel/setEffort slash-inject (MR0e verdict works), resume uses --continue. runAdapterConformance(adapter) drives the full lifecycle + asserts the contract shape, returning a structured report — used by tests with a stub (deterministic, no live model) and by integration checks with a real adapter. The generic pool (MRr-pool-multi) + bridge (MRr-bridge) + the Codex/Gemini adapters build ON this contract. Satisfies brief §3 token adapter-contract-ok.
